headline.text: Kotak Neo was live for 🔴 [Live] Commodities Update | US Data Fuels Metals | 6th July: 🔴 [Live] Commodities Update | US Data Fuels Metals | 6th July This show helps commodity traders understand where the key MCX contracts stand each morning, which levels matter the most, and how smart money may be positioning itself behind the scenes — across gold, silver, crude oil, natural gas, and base metals. Through a combination of technical analysis, global macro, physical-market fundamentals, intermarket flows, and Open Interest analytics, we aim to deliver actionable insight — not market noise. In this video, we cover: ✅ Overnight global cues and how they set the tone for the MCX session ✅ Technical structure, trend outlook, and key chart patterns across precious metals, energy, and base metals ✅ Macro drivers — the US dollar, US yields, central-bank policy (US Fed, RBI), and West Asia / geopolitical risk ✅ Fundamental factors — supply-demand balances, inventories, OPEC+ cues, and physical-market signals ✅ Intermarket linkages — how the dollar index, crude, equities, and bond yields pull commodities together ✅ Intraday Open Interest, Implied Volatility, and where option writers are positioned ✅ Critical support & resistance levels to track through the session ✅ Risk-managed intraday and positional strategies What you'll learn: How to read the morning setup through both the chart and the macro lens Which global data and events matter for commodities today How intermarket flows (USD, yields, crude) feed into MCX prices How to interpret Open Interest and volatility for sharper entries and exits Risk-first trade ideas with clearly defined levels If you're a commodity trader or investor looking for high-quality, logic-driven analysis that connects the chart to the bigger macro picture, this channel is built for you. We focus on live data, real-time market insights, and practical learning to help you stay ahead of the curve. 🎯 Watch till the end for a complete morning commodity breakdown and actionable strategy. Like, Share & Subscribe for regular commodity market insights. 🎙️ New to streaming or looking to level up? Check out StreamYard and get ₹740 discount! 😍 https://lnkd.in/d4TUN6Gn

headline.text: Dr.Raghunandan G H, Niharika Ranjan, and 4 other connections reacted to a post: Engineering meets professional services. And Atria IT meets Grant Thornton. Atria Institute of Technology, Bengaluru is proud to congratulate Prithvi Prabhu Pani, Dept. of CSE, on securing a placement at Grant Thornton, one of the world's leading organisations in audit, tax, and advisory services with a presence across more than 140 countries. A Computer Science graduate stepping into Grant Thornton is a reflection of something important: the lines between technology and professional services have dissolved completely. Today, the best consulting, audit, and advisory firms in the world run on technology talent and they know it. Prithvi's placement is a testament to the breadth of opportunity available to Atria's CSE graduates. The world is not just looking for engineers who can code. It is looking for engineers who can think, solve, and communicate across complex business environments. That is precisely what our program is designed to develop. Hearty congratulations, Prithvi. Grant Thornton gains a sharp mind and Atria gains another proud chapter. 📍 Atria Institute of Technology, Bengaluru | NAAC A++ | NBA Accredited | Autonomous Institution | VTU Affiliated | Est. 2000 | 🌐 atria.edu.in

successToastText.text: Thanks. Your feedback helps us improve your notifications.

headline.text: New from LinkedIn Guide to Creating in Create Possibilities: Create Opportunities Through Your Content

Mahima Hans posted: The skills that got people hired in 2020 are not the skills getting people hired in 2026. A few things I've personally seen Hiring Managers are expecting out of candidates now- → Actually knowing how to use AI tools, not copy-pasting answers, but being able to debug and explain AI-generated code line by line. → System design thinking, even at a junior level. AI is closing the gap on routine coding, which means the bar for "what makes you valuable" has moved up for everyone, freshers included. There are 3 more I go deep on in my latest video, including the one that's quietly become the biggest differentiator between two equally skilled engineers. Watch the complete video here- https://lnkd.in/d_7qwQE7

successToastText.text: Thanks. Your feedback helps us improve your notifications.

headline.text: Kushal Gayekwad posted: After an incredible journey as a Support Engineer at Zerto, acquired by HPE, I’ve decided to move on to the next chapter of my career. I’m truly grateful for the opportunities, challenges, and experiences that have helped me grow both professionally and personally. Working alongside talented colleagues and learning from such a dynamic environment has been invaluable. A heartfelt thank you to everyone who has been part of this journey—I look forward to staying connected. As I begin this new chapter, I’m excited to embrace new opportunities, continue learning, and take on fresh challenges. Here’s to growth and new beginnings!

successToastText.text: Thanks. Your feedback helps us improve your notifications.
